data.body:
PASS 1 review (E4 — assumption/premise verification) of the "Document reusable machinery API reference" doc ticket.

METHOD: every codebase assertion in the plan was probed with Read/Grep (no reliance on training knowledge).

VERIFIED (PASS — affirmations):
- Signing surface — read src/rebar/signing.py: all 8 named functions exist with the asserted shapes (sign_manifest, verify_signature, verify_record, signing_key, key_fingerprint, compute_signature, parse_manifest, head_sha). The manifest format (JSON array of non-empty strings), HMAC-SHA256 + PAYLOAD_VERSION=1 sorted-key compact-JSON canonicalisation, REBAR_SIGNING_KEY/.signing-key custody, certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned verdict vocab, SIGNATURE event + last-writer-wins replay, and head_sha audit binding all match the source verbatim. ✓
- LLM workflow runtime — read src/rebar/llm/runner.py: Runner protocol (run/preflight), RunRequest with every field the plan names (mode, execution_mode, output_schema, extra_tools — plus output_strategy/thinking), get_runner, PydanticAIRunner, and the structured-output stack (structured.py exists). ✓
- Workflow executor — src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py defines run_workflow(doc, inputs, *, run_id=..., target_ticket=..., ...) and the RunResult dataclass. inputs/run_id are real params (note: dry_run is NOT a run_workflow param — see finding). ✓ (with caveat)
- Prompt/contract model — read src/rebar/llm/prompts.py: get_prompt, resolve_prompt, the closed FRONT_MATTER_KEYS contract (inputs/outputs/execution_mode/category), .rebar/prompts/<id>.md project>built-in override resolution, the derived index + regenerate_prompt_index + `python -m rebar.llm.prompts` CI drift gate. ✓
- Output-schema seam — src/rebar/llm/findings.py defines validate_structured(...) and resolves file citations against the real repo; src/rebar/llm/completion.py defines verify_completion (the single-call example). ✓

FINDINGS:

- [ ] The "Why" section's load-bearing premise — "there is no single developer-facing API REFERENCE describing the reuse surface itself ... This ticket creates that reference" — is FALSE against the current codebase: docs/reuse-surface.md ALREADY EXISTS and substantially covers all four proposed sections (signing, LLM runtime, prompt/contract, output-schema seam), is written for human+LLM audiences, cross-links llm-framework.md, and even asserts source-verification. The plan would re-create an existing doc. Reframe the ticket as AUDIT/UPDATE/COMPLETE the existing docs/reuse-surface.md (verify-no-drift, fill gaps) rather than CREATE a new reference, and update AC#1 (which says "A developer-facing API reference exists (new doc ... e.g. docs/reuse-surface.md ...)") to acknowledge the existing file.
  - criteria: [E4]
  - location: plan "Why" section + Acceptance Criteria line 1; evidence file docs/reuse-surface.md
  - evidence: ["Why: 'there is no single developer-facing API REFERENCE ... This ticket creates that reference'", "docs/reuse-surface.md exists with header 'Reusable machinery — developer API reference' covering signing/runtime/prompt/output-schema and cross-linking llm-framework.md"]

- [ ] AC line 2's worked-example assertion uses sign_manifest/verify_signature for "a NEW gate reusing" the signing surface, but verify_signature(ticket_id, *, repo_root=None) does its own id-resolve + reduce + verify_record internally (it is not a low-level building block for a custom gate) — a new gate that signs a different manifest convention would more likely reuse compute_signature/verify_record/signing_key directly. Confirm the worked example documents the ACTUAL reuse seam (verify_record/compute_signature for a custom gate) rather than implying verify_signature is parameterizable for a new gate's manifest.
  - criteria: [E4]
  - location: Acceptance Criteria line 2 ("a worked example of a NEW gate reusing sign_manifest/verify_signature")
  - evidence: ["src/rebar/signing.py:362 verify_signature resolves+reduces the ticket and calls verify_record on state['signature'] — the pure reusable primitives are verify_record/compute_signature/signing_key", "the existing docs/reuse-surface.md §1 already frames reuse around compute_signature/verify_record + the convention 'plan-review: PASS' manifest line"]

- [ ] Scope item 2 lists "run_workflow (the declarative-IR executor: inputs/run_id/dry_run...)". There is no dry_run parameter on run_workflow (signature: doc, inputs, *, run_id, target_ticket, repo_root, scripted_registry, agent_runner, recorder, secrets) — dry-run, if it exists, is a workflow-doc/step concept, not a run_workflow arg. Either drop dry_run from the scope description or locate and name the real dry-run mechanism so the doc doesn't assert a non-existent parameter.
  - criteria: [E4]
  - location: plan "What / Scope" item 2 ("run_workflow (the declarative-IR executor: inputs/run_id/dry_run, RunResult, step kinds)")
  - evidence: ["src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py:584 run_workflow(...) has no dry_run parameter; its kwargs are run_id/target_ticket/repo_root/scripted_registry/agent_runner/recorder/secrets"]

affirmations: [E4] for the signing surface, LLM runtime (Runner/RunRequest/get_runner/PydanticAIRunner/structured.py), prompt/contract model (get_prompt/resolve_prompt/front-matter contract/override resolution/CI drift gate), and output-schema seam (validate_structured/findings citation resolution/verify_completion) — all named symbols and files resolve to real, accurately-described source.
